identity.title: Workflow Cannon Idea Planning System
identity.summary: Implement a command-backed dashboard flow that turns an Ideas row into a planner-chat session, a reviewed and accepted PlanArtifact with WBS, and optionally finalized Task Engine tasks.

goals: G1: Clicking Plan this starts or resumes exactly one durable planning session for the selected idea.
G2: start-idea-planning owns canonical idea loading, idempotency, session start/resume, and small prompt generation.
G3: update-idea-planning-session owns durable planning session transitions.
G4: PlanArtifact drafts are linked to ideas as activeDraftPlanArtifact and do not overwrite accepted linkedPlanArtifact until acceptance.
G5: Review is profile-aware, warnings do not block acceptance, and blockers prevent acceptance.
G6: Dashboard actions are derived from one tested lifecycle-state helper.
G7: Accepted WBS rows finalize into one task draft each with dependency resolution and provenance.
G8: Legacy build-plan remains compatibility-only and does not confuse the primary dashboard flow.
nonGoals: Do not make chat transcripts the source of truth.
Do not auto-finalize tasks immediately after plan acceptance.
Do not let the dashboard own planning business logic.
Do not replace Task Engine with PlanArtifact.
Do not implement legacy build-plan import before the primary planner-chat path is stable.

userStories.iWant: to click Plan this on an idea
userStories.soThat: I can start a focused brainstorming session without manually composing commands or prompts

userStories.iWant: the planning session to produce a reviewed PlanArtifact with WBS
userStories.soThat: I can approve a durable plan before execution tasks are created

userStories.iWant: the dashboard to show the idea's planning lifecycle state
userStories.soThat: I always know whether to resume, review, accept, finalize, or view tasks

userStories.iWant: one WBS row to map predictably to one task draft
userStories.soThat: I can finalize accepted plans reliably with traceable provenance

valueAssessment.impact: High: creates the core idea-to-plan-to-WBS workflow and removes ambiguity between planner-chat, PlanArtifact, dashboard, and legacy build-plan paths.
valueAssessment.rationale: The plan locks command ownership, session state transitions, derived dashboard lifecycle state, acceptance gating, and finalize-to-task provenance.

riskAssessment.description: State drift between idea status, session status, plan status, review result, and finalize result.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Implement deriveIdeaPlanningLifecycleState and invariant tests before broad UI rendering.

riskAssessment.description: Prompt behavior is treated as enforcement rather than UX guidance.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Back every critical prompt instruction with command validation and review/accept/finalize gates.

riskAssessment.description: Replanning could overwrite the current accepted plan too early.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Use both linkedPlanArtifact and activeDraftPlanArtifact, promoting only on acceptance.

riskAssessment.description: Finalize dependency mapping could produce broken task dependencies.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Use two-pass dependency resolution and block subset finalization when dependencies are unselected.

technicalImpact.compatibilityNotes: Legacy build-plan remains available but is demoted from the primary Ideas dashboard path. Legacy import is documented/deferred.
technicalImpact.migrationImpact: Ideas need fields or equivalent metadata for linkedPlanArtifact, activeDraftPlanArtifact, previousPlanArtifacts, and planningChatSession. Existing ideas without these fields should derive open state.

architecture.overview: Command-layer ownership should sit behind a thin dashboard. start-idea-planning starts/resumes durable sessions and returns a compact prompt. update-idea-planning-session owns session transitions. PlanArtifact commands own draft, review, accept, and finalize. Dashboard renders actions from deriveIdeaPlanningLifecycleState.

decisions.rationale: Keeps dashboard prompts small and centralizes lifecycle logic.
Makes session transitions durable, inspectable, and reusable.

decisions.decision: Dashboard uses a derived lifecycle-state reducer.
decisions.rationale: Prevents contradictory actions while preserving flexible persistence.
Protects accepted plan lineage during replanning.

decisions.decision: One WBS row maps to one task draft in v1.
decisions.rationale: Maximizes traceability and simplifies finalize behavior.

diagrams.title: Idea planning lifecycle
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[Ideas row] --> B[start-idea-planning]
  B --> C[planner-chat]
  C --> D[draft-plan-artifact]
  D --> E[review-plan-artifact]
  E -->|blockers| C
  E -->|passed/warnings| F[accept-plan-artifact]
  F --> G[linkedPlanArtifact accepted]
  G --> H[finalize-plan-to-phase dry-run]
  H --> I[finalize persist]
  I --> J[Task Engine tasks]
diagrams.caption: Command-backed flow from idea to accepted plan and optional task creation.

uiUxDirection.summary: The Ideas row should expose Plan this, Resume planning, Review, Accept, Finalize, View tasks, or View plan based only on deriveIdeaPlanningLifecycleState. Plan this opens a compact command-backed planner-chat prompt. Legacy wizard is moved behind Advanced/Legacy.

criticalPaths: Plan this creates or resumes one session
Session progresses active -> draft_ready -> needs_revision/approval_ready -> completed
Draft plan is linked as activeDraftPlanArtifact
Derived lifecycle state prevents contradictory dashboard actions
Finalize maps one WBS row to one task with dependency resolution and provenance
outOfScopeTesting: Visual design polish beyond required dashboard state/action rendering

implementationGuidance: Implement start-idea-planning and update-idea-planning-session before broad dashboard changes.
Keep dashboard logic thin; it should call commands and render derived state.
Use the current PlanArtifact schema enum for identity.planningType: new-feature.
Pass review profile full-feature when reviewing this plan; do not put full-feature into identity.planningType unless schema changes.
Do not store the full chat transcript as plan truth.
Use clientMutationId on mutating commands for idempotency.
whatNotToDo: Do not mark a planning session completed when a draft is merely saved.
Do not auto-finalize tasks after acceptance.
Do not overwrite linkedPlanArtifact during replanning before acceptance.
Do not let warnings block acceptance.
Do not let selected WBS rows finalize when they depend on unselected WBS rows.
Do not implement legacy import before the primary path is stable.
assumptions: Workspace-kit commands can be added to the planning/ideas command surface.
The dashboard can call these commands and open/prefill Cursor chat with returned prompt text.
Existing PlanArtifact lifecycle commands can be extended without replacing Task Engine.
Review profile can be supplied separately as full-feature even though identity.planningType is new-feature.

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 111 — Command-backed planning entry
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Establish real command ownership for Plan this and session start/update, then generate a small prompt.

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 112 — Planner chat and dashboard entry
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Wire dashboard and prompt/playbook behavior after command contracts exist.

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 113 — PlanArtifact draft and lineage
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Persist drafts with idea provenance and protect accepted-plan lineage.

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 114 — Review gate
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Make review deterministic and profile-aware before acceptance UI is completed.

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 115 — Acceptance gate
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Promote active drafts to accepted linked plans only after explicit acceptance.

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 116 — Dashboard lifecycle
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Render lifecycle actions from the derived-state helper and display plan/WBS details.

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 117 — Finalize to tasks
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Compile accepted WBS into task drafts with dependency resolution and idempotent persistence.

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 118 — Legacy cleanup
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Demote legacy planning and document migration without blocking the primary path.

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 119 — Reliability and audit
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Add invariant tests, generation handling, actionable errors, and audit metadata.
